assertions.assertion: Code fetches the discovery document to resolve the introspection endpoint rather than hardcoding it
Code calls IntrospectTokenAsync (or POSTs to the introspection endpoint) with the API's ClientId and ClientSecret for authentication
assertions.rationale: Standard introspection flow known from RFC 7662

assertions.assertion: Code checks the IsActive property (or 'active' field) of the introspection response to determine if the token is valid
assertions.rationale: Standard introspection response handling per RFC 7662

assertions.assertion: Response explains that reference tokens are opaque identifiers (not self-contained JWTs) and require server-side validation via introspection
Response mentions that reference tokens enable immediate revocation unlike JWTs which are valid until expiry
assertions.rationale: While generally known, the explicit connection between reference tokens and immediate revocation vs JWT expiry may not be consistently stated
